brands.name: Comfortmaker

structural_overview: Carrier Global is the largest and most architecturally complex brand family in the residential HVAC market. The family breaks into three distinct diagnostic worlds: (1) Carrier/Bryant/Payne conventional 24V equipment using 2-digit or 3-digit major.minor LED codes on the furnace board; (2) ICP-family equipment (Comfortmaker, Day & Night, Heil, Tempstar, Arcoaire, KeepRite) using simpler single-digit flash codes with 6+X soft-lockout subcodes on legacy SmartValve platforms; (3) Infinity/Evolution communicating systems with two-part wall-control codes, inverter/VFD diagnostic families, and board-level Status Code 16 variants. Same number often means different things across platforms — Code 6 has at least 4 different meanings, Code 16 has 3 different meanings on communicating equipment. Platform identification is critical. Public documentation coverage is strong on conventional boards and the 186CNV/284ANV inverter family, partial on communicating fan coils and legacy communicating outdoor, and thin on the complete Connex wall-control event dictionary (some event numbers require dealer portal access). The Infinity/Evolution Connex wall control also surfaces commissioning-time diagnostics (e.g., 'Indoor unit not found', 'Outdoor unit not found') during initial system discovery that are separate from runtime fault codes — a tech troubleshooting a running system should not see these, and a tech seeing these during install should not look for them in runtime fault history.

field_quirks.quirk: Same code number means different things across Carrier Global platforms
field_quirks.applies_to: All platforms — cross-generation conflicts are the #1 diagnostic trap
field_quirks.reason: Code 6 alone has 4+ meanings: ignition proving failure on Recent ICP IGC, failure to ignite/flame lost on SmartValve legacy single-stage, soft lockout status on ICP Gen 1 two-stage, active ignition/flame-proving failure on ICP Gen 2/3. Code 16 has 3 meanings across communicating fan coils, legacy communicating outdoor, and Greenspeed/Extreme platforms. Platform identification is mandatory before trusting any code meaning.

field_quirks.quirk: 24V-trained tech often treats Infinity/Evolution wall control like a thermostat
field_quirks.reason: The Connex wall control is not just sending Y/W/G calls — it's part of the ABCD communication network. A comm fault can take down system behavior in a way that looks like a 'bad thermostat' on first glance, when the real issue is bus wiring, device loading, or a failed board elsewhere on the network.

field_quirks.quirk: Board LEDs and wall-control events are not interchangeable on Infinity/Evolution
field_quirks.reason: On legacy communicating outdoor (24ANB/25HCB), tech sees local board Status Code 16. On 186CNV/284ANV inverter outdoor, the local clue is COMM light OFF or the matrix display, while the richer two-part codes live at the wall control. Treating 'no board flash code' as 'no fault' is a trap on the inverter platform.

field_quirks.quirk: Model-plug logic is a trap during board replacements
field_quirks.applies_to: All Carrier/Bryant communicating outdoor and modulating furnace platforms
field_quirks.reason: A board replacement that would be straightforward on conventional 24V systems can create communicating faults or invalid-model behavior if the model plug is wrong, missing, or not transferred. Communicating outdoor manuals repeatedly call out the model plug as required.

field_quirks.quirk: Emergency operation mode can hide the real communicating architecture
field_quirks.applies_to: Communicating fan coils and 186CNV/284ANV inverter outdoor
field_quirks.reason: When communication is lost for more than 2 minutes, fan coils drop into emergency-operable mode with a common thermostat, and inverter outdoor enters emergency communicating mode. A tech from conventional systems can misread this as proof the system is 'basically a normal 24V setup.' Emergency mode is a degraded fallback, not the native control architecture.

field_quirks.quirk: Legacy variable-speed-only codes don't exist on 58SC0B
field_quirks.reason: Codes 15, 41, 42, 43, 44 are part of the older ECM/variable-speed logic families and are NOT present on the newer 58SC0B single-stage platform. A tech troubleshooting a 58SC0B shouldn't look for these codes.

field_quirks.quirk: Connex wall control firmware version affects how fault expansion codes are displayed in Last 10 Events history
field_quirks.applies_to: Infinity/Evolution Connex wall control — all platforms using Connex UI
field_quirks.reason: Per Carrier/Bryant PTC bulletins for Infinity/Evolution Extreme 24/26, after specific firmware updates, fault codes with different expansion codes are treated as distinct entries in Last 10 Events. Before the update, these may appear merged or behave differently. Two techs on different firmware versions may see the same fault cluster displayed differently. Additionally, updated firmware stores fault description messages in permanent memory to prevent incorrect messages after a wall-control reset. If a history screen looks wrong or inconsistent with equipment behavior, verify firmware version and consider applying the latest update before deeper diagnosis.

platforms.platform_description: Classic Carrier/Bryant/Payne variable-speed condensing furnace control board using a 2-digit LED flash code (first digit short flashes, second digit long flashes). Stores fault history automatically for 72 hours. Supports component test mode that flashes 11, 25, 41, 42 as operational indicators.
platforms.visual_identification: Single LED that flashes 2-digit codes (first digit short flashes, second digit long flashes). Shows CONTINUOUS OFF, CONTINUOUS ON, or RAPID FLASHING as status states. Supports component test mode when button is pressed.
platforms.platform_notes: Auto-resets limit lockout (13) and ignition lockout (14) after 3 hours. Gas heating lockout (21) does NOT auto-reset. Fault history stored for 72 hours or manually cleared. Codes 15, 41, 42, 43, 44 are variable-speed-only and are NOT present on 58SC0B single-stage successor.

codes.meaning: No control power indicated; check line and secondary power
diagnostic_path: Verify 115 VAC at L1/L2
Verify 24 VAC at SEC-1/SEC-2
Inspect interlock/transformer/board
codes.escalation: If both line and secondary voltage are present and LED remains off, control failure becomes likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Not a timed lockout; clears when power is restored.

codes.meaning: Control has 24 VAC power (normal powered state, no active flash code)
diagnostic_path: If unit is still inoperative, proceed to active operating sequence checks and stored code recall
codes.escalation: None by itself
codes.reset_behavior: Steady while powered

diagnostic_path: Verify polarity first
Verify furnace grounding
Verify supply wiring
codes.escalation: Persistent rapid flash with verified supply wiring points to control/wiring abnormality.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when polarity issue is corrected.

diagnostic_path: Run furnace through a full heating cycle if complaint is intermittent and no active code is present
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Stored codes erase after 72 hours of operation or by manual clear procedure.

root_causes: Normal response when powered during a heat call
Normal response when R-W opens during blower on-delay
Direct short in certain circuits can cause repetitive code-12 behavior
diagnostic_path: Verify whether unit was repowered mid-call before treating this as a fault
codes.escalation: None unless blower remains abnormal after the 90-second period.
codes.reset_behavior: Blower runs 90 seconds, then control resumes normal sequence.

root_causes: Limit or rollout switch open more than 3 minutes
10 successive limit trips during high heat
Dirty filter, weak blower, wrong airflow setup
Venting/combustion issue if rollout is involved
diagnostic_path: Determine whether the open device is main limit or rollout
Verify airflow/static/blower operation
Inspect venting/burner/heat-exchanger area if rollout is implicated
codes.escalation: Manual-reset rollout or recurring limit trip after airflow corrections indicates a harder safety/combustion problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-resets after 3 hours.

root_causes: No gas, weak flame sense, poor ground, igniter issue, burner carryover problem
diagnostic_path: Verify gas supply and valve state
Verify igniter operation
Verify flame-sense microamps and ground continuity
codes.escalation: Repeated 14 after normal gas and ignition hardware checks points toward control or valve problems.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-resets after 3 hours.

root_causes: Blower failed to communicate within 30 seconds in two successive heating cycles
diagnostic_path: Verify blower motor powers and communicates
Inspect harness/connections
Isolate ECM motor/module vs control with OEM blower/ECM procedures
codes.escalation: Repeated lockout after proven-good blower wiring/power strongly points to ECM module/motor or control failure.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-resets after 3 hours.
codes.source: Carrier 58-06T troubleshooting guide and ECM simulator bulletin
codes.field_consensus_notes: Variable-speed-only code; NOT present on 58SC0B single-stage successor.

root_causes: Stuck-closed main gas valve relay on control
Flame-sense loss after burner-on time (in some lockout scenarios)
diagnostic_path: Verify gas-valve wiring is correct (blue to M / green-yellow to C)
Isolate miswire/short vs relay failure using OEM steps
If lockout occurs only when pressure switch makes, suspect control
codes.escalation: If wiring is correct and code still flashes under proven conditions, control board becomes likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Does NOT auto-reset.
codes.source: Carrier 58-06T and mirrored OEM 58MVC troubleshooting excerpt

root_causes: Flame sensed while gas valve is de-energized
diagnostic_path: Shut off external gas supply and observe whether code clears
If code clears, suspect leaking gas valve
If code persists with gas off, isolate flame-sensing/control circuit
codes.escalation: Persistent false flame with gas physically shut off moves suspicion toward board/flame circuit.
codes.reset_behavior: Inducer runs until fault clears.
codes.source: Carrier 58-3T and mirrored OEM 58MVC troubleshooting excerpt

root_causes: Moisture/debris in tubing or trap on condensing units
diagnostic_path: Verify switch state with inducer off
Inspect tubing for blockage/water
Prove switch operation with manometer if needed
codes.escalation: If switch state is correct but board still reports fault, control/input issue becomes possible.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when proper switch-open condition is restored.
codes.source: Carrier 58-3T summary and Carrier 58-06T guide flow
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: 58SC0B successor uses 23.1 format for stuck main pressure switch.

root_causes: Board fuse blown during service or install
diagnostic_path: Isolate low-voltage wiring
Inspect thermostat, outdoor, and accessory circuits
Replace fuse only after fault isolation
codes.escalation: Repeated fuse failures after field wiring isolation raise suspicion of board/transformer issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after fuse replacement and fault correction.

root_causes: Thermostat call with setup switch on
Incompatible 58UVB model-plug recognition on older board date codes
diagnostic_path: Verify model plug
Verify setup-switch position
Verify no call is present during setup functions
codes.escalation: If plug/switching are correct and fault persists, control replacement becomes more likely.
codes.reset_behavior: If flashes only four times at power-up, board is defaulting to stored model selection; persistent flashing indicates active setup fault.

codes.meaning: High-heat pressure switch or relay did not close or reopened
root_causes: Condensate issues or vent restrictions can indirectly create high-fire proving complaints
diagnostic_path: Verify high-heat proving sequence and switch closure
Verify relay/gas-valve wiring
Inspect venting/condensate path
codes.escalation: Repeated code after proofing checks points toward relay/control fault.
codes.reset_behavior: Active fault clears when proper proving returns.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Legacy 58MVP Code 31 is high-heat-specific staging logic. 58SC0B successor Code 31.1 is broader general pressure-switch fault since it's single-stage.

codes.meaning: Low-heat pressure switch, draft safeguard, or auxiliary limit (downflow only) did not close or reopened
root_causes: Draft safeguard/auxiliary-limit opening on specific configurations
diagnostic_path: Verify which switch in the chain is actually open
Inspect tubing/condensate path and low-fire draft
Follow OEM draft/pressure procedure
codes.escalation: Repeated 32 on condensing units with correct switch operation often points to marginal draft or trap/vent issues.
codes.reset_behavior: Retry logic varies with sequence stage; diagnose active condition.

diagnostic_path: Separate airflow problem from rollout/combustion problem
Check static, temp rise, blower operation, filter, wheel
Inspect rollout/manual-reset switch and vent path
codes.escalation: If open longer than 3 minutes changes to lockout code 13; rollout/manual-reset event is a harder safety problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Blower runs 4 minutes or until switch remakes, whichever is longer; escalates to 13 if open >3 minutes.

diagnostic_path: Measure flame current in µA
Check sensor, ground path, burner carryover, gas pressure
Verify gas valve operation and inlet pressure
codes.escalation: Repeated flame loss after successful ignition can point to rollout/combustion instability or board/flame-rectification issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Control retries three more times before lockout 14.

root_causes: Communication failure between blower and control
diagnostic_path: Verify line voltage and harness
Inspect blower wheel drag/binding
Isolate ECM module/motor vs control with OEM motor procedures
codes.escalation: If code 15 follows, event has escalated to blower motor lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: May escalate to 15 lockout after repeated failure.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Variable-speed-only code; NOT present on 58SC0B single-stage successor.

root_causes: Inducer RPM out of valid range
Lost inducer RPM signal for 5 seconds
diagnostic_path: Verify inducer operation and RPM
Inspect venting/combustion-air path
Inspect motor wiring
codes.escalation: Repeated code with free venting and correct power points toward inducer or board failure.
codes.reset_behavior: Active fault clears when inducer feedback normalizes.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Variable-speed-only code; NOT present on 58SC0B single-stage successor.

codes.meaning: Low-heat pressure switch open while high-heat pressure switch is closed
diagnostic_path: Check vent sag, condensate trap/line, tubing, pressure switch circuit, draft timing
codes.escalation: If vent/drain path is right and switch circuit checks out, board or pressure-sensing integrity issue is next.
codes.reset_behavior: Fault logic tied to pressure-switch state relationship.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Variable-speed-only code; NOT present on 58SC0B single-stage successor.

root_causes: Calculated blower speed below 250 RPM or above 1300 RPM
ECM module or tach/rpm feedback issue
High static causing blower control problems
diagnostic_path: Verify blower wheel spins freely and airflow path is not excessively restricted
Inspect ECM wiring/module and blower RPM feedback
Follow OEM blower calibration/ECM diagnostic procedure
codes.escalation: If code 41 is also present, focus on blower communication/module failure.
codes.reset_behavior: Sequence-dependent; heating mode defaults and fallback behavior depend on operating mode.
codes.source: Mirrored OEM/manual references for Carrier 58MVP fault section
codes.field_consensus_notes: Variable-speed-only code; NOT present on 58SC0B single-stage successor.

diagnostic_path: Cycle power
Verify flame-sense circuit and gas-valve relay behavior
If code repeats, replace control
codes.escalation: Repeated code 45 after clean power reset is high-confidence control failure.
codes.reset_behavior: Lockout behavior; reset by power cycle, but repeated recurrence indicates failed control. Auto-resets after 1 hour.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: 58SC0B successor splits into 45.1/45.2/45.3 granular subcodes.

platforms.platform_description: Newer single-stage non-communicating Carrier-family furnace control using a 3-digit display with major.minor code format (e.g., 14.1, 45.1). Stores last 7 faults. Descendant of 58MVP-era logic but with more granular subcodes. Variable-speed-only codes 15, 41, 42, 43, 44 are NOT present on this single-stage platform.
platforms.visual_identification: 3-digit display showing codes in format like 14.1, 31.1, 45.1 (major.minor). Pushbutton navigation for fault history recall. Shows the last 7 faults.
platforms.platform_notes: Faults stored 72 hours or cleared manually. Auto-reset after 3 hours on 13.1/14.1 lockout. 21.1 gas heating lockout requires power cycle after 5 minutes.

diagnostic_path: If complaint is intermittent, run heat/cool sequence and re-check the last-7-fault menu
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Faults remain in memory for 72 hours of powered operation; manual clear procedure also available.
codes.source: PG96VTA install/service manual and Carrier/Bryant 3-digit service-label documentation

root_causes: Normal operation if unit is repowered during a heat call or blower-on-delay interval
diagnostic_path: Verify recent power interruption before treating as a defect
codes.escalation: None unless the blower stays abnormal beyond the normal purge interval.
codes.reset_behavior: Normal 90-second blower run, then control resumes normal sequence.

root_causes: Limit or flame rollout switch open longer than 3 minutes
Low airflow, blocked filter/coil, blower-speed mismatch, rollout-related combustion problem
diagnostic_path: Identify whether the open device is main limit, draft safeguard, rollout, or blocked-vent branch
Verify airflow/static/blower first if it is a limit event
codes.escalation: Manual-reset rollout or repeated limit cycling after airflow correction indicates higher-risk combustion or HX issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-resets after 3 hours.

root_causes: System failed to ignite gas and prove flame in 4 attempts
No gas, poor flame sense, grounding issue, ignition hardware problem
diagnostic_path: Verify gas supply
Verify HSI and valve operation
Verify flame-sense current and grounding
codes.escalation: Repeated 14.1 after normal gas and ignition checks points toward board/valve issues.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-resets after 3 hours.

codes.meaning: Gas heating lockout; 24 VAC sensed on gas valve when it should not be
diagnostic_path: Verify unwanted voltage at gas valve
Compare wiring to schematic
Replace board only after field miswire is ruled out
codes.escalation: Strong control/relay concern if wiring is correct.
codes.reset_behavior: Turn off power and wait 5 minutes to retry.

diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before inducer run
Inspect tubing and ports
Replace switch only after tubing is proven clear
codes.escalation: Persistent false-closed reading with proven tubing and switch state points toward board input fault.
codes.reset_behavior: Active fault clears when pressure-switch startup proofing normalizes.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Legacy 58MVP uses Code 23 (no decimal) for same subsystem.

codes.meaning: Low-voltage fuse needs replacement / fuse open
root_causes: Thermostat, outdoor contactor coil, humidifier, accessory, or harness short
diagnostic_path: Isolate low-voltage branches
Inspect thermostat/outdoor/accessory wiring
Replace fuse only after short path is corrected
codes.escalation: Repeated 24.1 means real short, not nuisance fuse failure.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when fuse is replaced and the short is removed.

codes.meaning: Open main pressure switch / pressure switch did not function properly
root_causes: Sagged vent, plugged trap, water in tubing, marginal inducer, wind effects
diagnostic_path: Verify inducer operation and actual proving path
Inspect condensate drain, tubing, and venting
Verify switch function
codes.escalation: If HSI glows with inducer disconnected, manual says shut furnace down immediately.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when proper proving returns.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Legacy 58MVP Code 31 is high-heat-specific staging logic (different meaning on staged equipment).

codes.meaning: Switch opened in the main limit / main limit branch open
root_causes: Dirty filter, oversized temperature rise, closed registers, weak motor/capacitor on non-ECM applications
diagnostic_path: Verify airflow/static and filter
Verify blower operation and heat rise
Inspect limit circuit
codes.escalation: Repeated main-limit opening after airflow correction suggests switch or HX/combustion concern.
codes.reset_behavior: Transitions into 13.1 if lockout criteria are met.

root_causes: Adequate flame sense not detected during trial for ignition
Dirty flame sensor, weak ground, low gas pressure, bad gas valve, burner carryover issue
diagnostic_path: Verify flame is actually lighting
Verify flame-sense current and clean sensor
Verify gas pressure and ground continuity
codes.escalation: Repeated 34.1 after proper flame current and gas checks points toward control/valve faults.
codes.reset_behavior: Repeated proving failures escalate to 14.1 lockout.
codes.source: Carrier-family public snippet on newer major.minor platform

root_causes: Sometimes surfaced after repeated false lockouts
diagnostic_path: Verify line and low-voltage supply
Verify no induced miswire/backfeed
Replace control if fault persists
codes.escalation: Board-level escalation.
codes.reset_behavior: Not documented in public snippets as a timed auto-reset; treat as persistent until corrected/reset.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Legacy 58MVP Code 45 is broad control lockout. 58SC0B splits into 45.1/45.2/45.3 (this is 45.1). Additional subcodes 45.2 (gas valve relay won't close) and 45.3 (EEPROM) may exist but weren't cleanly verifiable from public sources in this pass.

platforms.platform_description: Carrier/Bryant 38TDB WeatherMaker / Infinity 18 two-speed air conditioner outdoor control board. Outdoor-board LED language, keep separate from furnace records.
platforms.visual_identification: Single LED on outdoor control board showing flash patterns — single count, or compound patterns like '3 pause 4', '5 pause 1', '5 pause 2'. Constant flash is standby, constant light is board failure.
platforms.platform_notes: Outdoor-only codes — NOT furnace codes. Do not merge into indoor-furnace lookup logic.

root_causes: No active Y call; normal idle state
diagnostic_path: Verify thermostat demand and low-voltage call before chasing refrigeration faults
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Normal standby state.

diagnostic_path: Verify staging call if expected high speed is absent
codes.escalation: None by itself
codes.reset_behavior: Follows active demand.

diagnostic_path: Verify thermostat/control staging if expected high speed never appears
codes.escalation: None by itself
codes.reset_behavior: Follows active demand.

diagnostic_path: Inspect sensor resistance and harness
Verify sensor placement
codes.escalation: If sensor values are correct at the board, control-board input fault becomes more likely.
codes.reset_behavior: No public reset timing documented in install/start-up manual.

diagnostic_path: Verify coil-sensor resistance and wiring
Verify mounting/location
codes.escalation: If sensor checks good at board, suspect control input issue.
codes.reset_behavior: No public reset timing documented in install/start-up manual.

root_causes: Poor sensor placement (manual warns nuisance 'out of frame' faults if outdoor-air thermistor is not placed properly under the base pan)
diagnostic_path: Verify sensor placement
Verify actual thermistor resistance vs temperature
codes.escalation: Repeated fault after proper placement points toward sensor/control issue.
codes.reset_behavior: No public reset timing documented in install/start-up manual.

diagnostic_path: Verify actual operating pressures and airflow
Inspect charge/restriction path
codes.escalation: Repeated LPS trips under normal load point toward leak/restriction/system issue.
codes.reset_behavior: No public timing/reset detail documented in Table 5.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Refrigerant-side issue is more common than bad switch.

diagnostic_path: Inspect outdoor airflow and fan
Verify pressures and charge
codes.escalation: Repeated HPS trips under clean-airflow conditions indicate deeper refrigerant/control issues.
codes.reset_behavior: No public timing/reset detail documented in Table 5.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Condenser airflow is the first thing to check.

root_causes: Compressor protective trip / internal protection event
Can be secondary to pressure problems or electrical/mechanical compressor stress
diagnostic_path: Verify supply voltage and compressor condition
Verify whether an HPS/LPS or thermistor issue preceded it
codes.escalation: Repeated protective trips point toward compressor or system abnormality.
codes.reset_behavior: No public timing/reset detail documented in Table 5.

codes.code_identifier: Constant light / no pause / no flash
diagnostic_path: Verify board power and wiring
Replace board if supply conditions are correct
codes.escalation: Board-level escalation.
codes.reset_behavior: Not documented as a timed auto-reset in install/start-up manual.

platforms.platform_description: ICP single-stage IGC board used on N9MSB/N9MSE/N9MS family and sibling badge-equivalent Comfortmaker, Day & Night, Heil, Tempstar, Arcoaire, KeepRite models. Uses single-LED flash codes (1-10) plus compound codes (1+2, 6+1). Merged platform — 'Recent' and 'Intermediate' generations share the same service-label legend per primary-source verification.
platforms.visual_identification: Single LED board with heartbeat (bright-dim) for normal, single-digit flash counts 2-10 for faults, plus compound codes 1+2 and 6+1. ON SOLID indicates control lockout.
platforms.platform_notes: Auto-reset after 3 hours on 6+1 and 14-family lockouts. 9 flashes (internal software processor fault) was NOT cleanly verifiable from primary-source material and is NOT included.

codes.meaning: Control has 24 VAC power / normal powered state
root_causes: Normal standby or active normal operation with no stored fault recall in progress
diagnostic_path: Verify call for heat and live sequence if complaint persists with only heartbeat showing
codes.escalation: None by itself
codes.reset_behavior: Steady repeating heartbeat while powered normally.

codes.meaning: Secondary voltage fuse open / board not properly powered
diagnostic_path: Verify blower-door switch and line voltage
Verify 24 VAC at board
Isolate low-voltage short and inspect fuse
codes.escalation: Repeated fuse failure indicates a real low-voltage short.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when power/fuse issue is corrected.
codes.source: N9MSB/N9MSE service-label legend and troubleshooting flowchart

diagnostic_path: Reset power
Verify flame-sense circuit, gas-valve relay behavior, and general board operation
codes.escalation: If code repeats after power reset, OEM directs replacement of control.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-reset after 1 hour; power reset clears immediately.
codes.source: N9MSB/N9MSE service-label legend and troubleshooting flowchart

diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before inducer starts
Inspect tubing and ports
Replace switch only after proving tubing/port integrity
codes.escalation: If switch state remains wrong with known-good tubing, switch or board input fault becomes more likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when startup proofing normalizes.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch did not close or reopened
root_causes: Low inlet gas pressure if LGPS used
Plugged trap/tubing and marginal inducer performance are common on nuisance repeats
diagnostic_path: Inspect venting, condensate, and tubing
Verify inducer operation and incoming voltage
Verify switch calibration and inlet gas pressure if LGPS is in play
codes.escalation: If pressure switch remains open after three successive trials, board locks out for 3 hours before retry.
codes.reset_behavior: Inducer shuts off for 15 minutes before retry; if HPS remains open for 1 minute after gas valve closes after three trials, control locks out for 3 hours before retry.
codes.source: ICP N9MSB service label, Figure 14, manual 440 04 4413 00 / service label rev. 338310-2 Rev F

codes.meaning: Limit circuit fault; indicates a limit or flame rollout is open
root_causes: Dirty filter or restricted duct system
diagnostic_path: Identify whether open device is main limit or rollout
Verify filter, duct restriction, blower wheel, blower motor/capacitor, and venting
Inspect switch wiring/connections and combustion-air path
codes.escalation: If flame-rollout is open, this is a combustion safety escalation; if switch remains open longer than 3 minutes, code changes to lockout 7.
codes.reset_behavior: Blower runs for 4 minutes or until open switch remakes, whichever is longer; if open less than 3 minutes code 4 continues until blower shuts off; if open longer than 3 minutes it becomes lockout 7.

codes.meaning: Abnormal flame-proving signal; flame proved while gas valve is de-energized
root_causes: Contaminated flame-sense path or grounding issues can contribute
diagnostic_path: Verify whether flame is actually present with valve de-energized
Isolate gas valve leakage/stuck-open condition
Inspect flame-sense path only after valve behavior is ruled out
codes.escalation: Persistent abnormal flame-proving with gas valve confirmed closed points toward control or sensing fault; actual gas leakage/stuck-open valve is a high-priority safety issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Inducer runs until the fault is cleared.

root_causes: Flame sensor must not be grounded
Gas valve defective or gas valve turned off
Inadequate flame carryover or rough ignition
diagnostic_path: Verify gas is on and inlet gas pressure is adequate
Verify HSI operation and burner carryover
Clean/check flame sensor and verify flame-sense microamps and control grounding
codes.escalation: Control retries and then escalates to 6+1 ignition lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: Control will try three more times before 6+1 ignition lockout occurs.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: On ICP SmartValve legacy single-stage, Code 6 is broader (failure to ignite OR flame sense lost during run). On ICP Gen 1 NTV6, Code 6 is soft lockout status.

codes.meaning: Limit circuit lockout; lockout occurs if limit or flame-rollout circuit stays open longer than 3 minutes
root_causes: Dirty filter, blocked coil, failed/slow blower, undersized duct, or actual combustion/rollout issue if rollout is the open device
diagnostic_path: Determine whether open device is main limit or rollout switch
Verify airflow, filter, blower operation, and temperature rise
Inspect burners, venting, and heat exchanger area if rollout is involved
codes.escalation: Rollout involvement is the hard-safety branch; repeated limit lockout after airflow correction points to deeper blower, heat-rise, or heat-exchanger/combustion issues.
codes.reset_behavior: Control auto-resets after 3 hours.
codes.source: N9MSB service-label troubleshooting page and N9MSE service-manual retrieval/service-label instructions
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: ICP SmartValve legacy single-stage Code 7 triggers at 2 minutes with 1-hour lockout (shorter threshold, shorter reset).

root_causes: False backfeed to GV lead and board relay failure are main branches once field miswire is ruled out
diagnostic_path: Verify whether 24 V is being sent to gas valve when it should not be
Compare gas-valve wiring to schematic and look for shorts/backfeed
Condemn board only after field wiring is proven correct
codes.escalation: Board/relay-level escalation if gas-valve circuit is wired correctly.
codes.reset_behavior: Control does not auto-reset; turn off power and wait 5 minutes to retry.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: ICP Gen 1 NTV6 two-stage Code 8 means HPS stuck closed (completely different).

root_causes: If twinned, improper low-voltage transformer phasing
diagnostic_path: Verify correct 115 V hot/neutral polarity
Verify chassis grounding
If twinned, verify 24 V phasing per twinning instructions
codes.escalation: Persistent polarity indication with verified incoming wiring points toward control-input abnormality.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when polarity/phasing issue is corrected.

root_causes: Normal response when power is restored during a call for heat or when R-W/W1 opens during blower on-delay
diagnostic_path: Verify whether there was a recent power interruption during a heat call
Verify blower clears after normal timed period before treating it as a fault
codes.escalation: None by itself unless blower operation stays abnormal beyond normal post-power-up interval.
codes.reset_behavior: Blower runs 90 seconds, then unit returns to normal heartbeat/no-fault display if no faults are present.
codes.source: N9MSB service-label page and N9MSB/N9MSE service-manual text describing the 90-second blower-only period

codes.meaning: Ignition lockout; system failed to ignite gas and prove flame in 4 attempts
root_causes: Same root-cause family as code 6: no gas, igniter issue, low flame current, poor ground, gas-valve problem, burner carryover issue
diagnostic_path: Troubleshoot active proving-failure path under code 6
Verify gas supply, igniter operation, and flame-sense current
Verify grounding and gas-valve operation
codes.escalation: Timed lockout state after repeated code-6 ignition-proving failures.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-resets after 3 hours; service label says refer back to status code 6.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: ICP SmartValve legacy single-stage 6+1 has 5-minute soft lockout (not 3-hour).

platforms.platform_description: Older Honeywell SmartValve-based ICP single-stage family with 6+X soft-lockout subcode architecture. Uses simpler flash codes with the 6+X compound codes identifying the last abnormal event that forced lockout. Different from Recent/Intermediate IGC platform — meanings for Code 6, 7, and reset behaviors differ.
platforms.visual_identification: Single LED with 6+X soft-lockout subcode architecture (6+1, 6+2, 6+3, 6+4). Uses Honeywell SmartValve with separate diagnostic indicator. 5-minute soft lockout instead of 3-hour like newer IGC.
platforms.platform_notes: 6+X soft-lockout architecture is defining feature. 5-minute soft lockout then retry. Code 8 exists in field reports but was NOT cleanly verifiable from primary SmartValve tables and is excluded.

diagnostic_path: Do not build logic around this code
Verify actual displayed pattern is not heartbeat or another multi-part code
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Not applicable.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch(es) closed when should be open
root_causes: Tubing issue causing false closed reading
diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before inducer start
Inspect tubing and vent-pressure connections
Verify switch is not welded closed
codes.escalation: Persistent false-closed reading with normal switch operation points toward board-input or wiring problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when startup proving state is normal.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch open when should be closed
root_causes: Blocked condensate path on condensing versions
diagnostic_path: Verify inducer operation and pressure proving
Inspect vent/condensate/tubing path
Verify switch calibration and closure
codes.escalation: Repeated proving failure after venting/tubing corrections points toward switch or board problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when proving state is corrected.

codes.meaning: Limit or rollout switch open, less than 2 minutes
root_causes: Main limit open from low airflow
diagnostic_path: Determine whether main limit or rollout is open
Verify airflow/filter/blower
Inspect combustion and burner area if rollout is involved
codes.escalation: If open condition persists beyond 2 minutes, progresses into code 7 soft lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: Active/live fault; if open longer than 2 minutes the platform advances to 7 soft lockout.

diagnostic_path: Verify whether flame is actually present when there is no call for gas
Inspect flame-sense circuit and grounding
Evaluate gas valve leakage
codes.escalation: Persistent out-of-sequence flame indication with gas shut off points toward control or sensing problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when false-flame condition is removed.

codes.meaning: Failure to ignite or flame sense lost while running
root_causes: This is the broad active fault before the board sorts the final lockout branch
diagnostic_path: Verify ignition sequence and gas supply
Verify flame-sense current and grounding
Determine whether failure was initial ignition or flame loss during run
codes.escalation: Repeated abnormal events move to 6+1, 6+2, 6+3, or 6+4 depending on last event that forced soft lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: Active/live abnormal-event fault; after repeated attempts/events system proceeds to soft lockout and shows a 6+X subcode.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: ICP Recent IGC Code 6 is narrower (ignition proving failure only).

codes.meaning: Soft lockout, maximum ignition trials reached
root_causes: Inability to prove flame in allotted trial sequence
diagnostic_path: Verify gas supply and gas valve state
Verify igniter warm-up and ignition
Verify flame-sense current and ground
codes.escalation: Soft-lockout branch specifically for repeated ignition-trial failure.
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout; public SmartValve narrative says control exits soft lockout after 5 minutes and begins another ignition sequence.
codes.source: Public N8MPN SmartValve-era diagnostic-code text and SmartValve ignition-sequence narrative
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: ICP Recent IGC 6+1 has 3-hour auto-reset (not 5 minutes).

codes.meaning: Soft lockout, flame-sense failure during run
diagnostic_path: Measure flame-sense current during run
Verify burner flame stability and ground path
Verify gas pressure under operation
codes.escalation: Indicates last abnormal event driving lockout was flame loss after successful ignition, not initial no-light.
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout; clears after 5 minutes and retries.
codes.source: SmartValve public narrative stating any combination of abnormal events puts control in soft lockout and flash code indicates last abnormal event

codes.meaning: Soft lockout, airflow-proving circuit open during run
diagnostic_path: Inspect pressure-switch tubing, venting, and condensate path
Verify inducer performance and switch operation under run conditions
codes.escalation: SmartValve subcode that can look like a simple pressure-switch complaint but specifically means last abnormal event that forced soft lockout was proving-circuit open during run.
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout; clears after 5 minutes and retries.
codes.source: SmartValve public narrative identifying airflow-proving-circuit-open-during-run as one of the abnormal events that define the final 6+X code

codes.meaning: Soft lockout, limit circuit open during run
diagnostic_path: Verify airflow, filter, blower, and temperature rise
Determine whether open event was main limit or rollout-related
codes.escalation: Identifies limit-circuit-open-during-run as final abnormal event that pushed board into soft lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: Soft lockout; clears after 5 minutes and retries.
codes.source: SmartValve public narrative identifying limit-circuit-open-during-run as one of the abnormal events that define the final 6+X code

codes.meaning: Soft lockout; limit or rollout switch open longer than 2 minutes
root_causes: Main limit staying open due to airflow problem
Rollout switch open from combustion issue
Manual-reset rollout remains a special case
diagnostic_path: Identify whether open device is main limit or rollout
Verify airflow/blower if limit-driven
Inspect burners/venting/combustion if rollout-driven
codes.escalation: Rollout involvement is the high-risk branch; persistent open longer than 2 minutes moves beyond live code-4 state into soft lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: 1-hour soft lockout per public N8MPN text summary; rollout switch requires manual reset.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: Recent IGC Code 7 triggers at 3 minutes with 3-hour auto-reset (different threshold, longer reset).

platforms.platform_description: Early ICP staged and variable-speed platform using Honeywell gas-valve diagnostic light architecture rather than later amber-board status system. Different from Gen 2/3 SmartValve and 9MVE platforms.
platforms.visual_identification: Diagnostic light on Honeywell gas valve (not on furnace control board). Short flash list: steady flash, then counts 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9. No 6+X subcode architecture on this generation.
platforms.platform_notes: Uses gas-valve diagnostic light, not board LED. Codes 1, 7, 10, and 6+X subcodes are NOT on this generation per public documentation.

diagnostic_path: Verify call type and sequence normally
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: N/A

root_causes: Switch not opening at call start
diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before inducer start
Inspect tubing and wiring
Confirm switch opens as call begins
codes.escalation: Replace switch only after tubing/port/wiring checks.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when fault is corrected and call restarts.

codes.meaning: Low-pressure switch stuck open, or manual ON/OFF switch in OFF position, or auxiliary limit open on upflow-only applications
root_causes: Vent restriction / inducer or tubing problems
diagnostic_path: Confirm gas-valve ON/OFF position
Verify aux limit continuity where applicable
Verify low-pressure switch closes during inducer run
codes.escalation: Escalate to inducer/venting/switch testing if switch still open under correct draft.
codes.reset_behavior: Retry after condition is corrected.
codes.source: OEM code table and sequence text

codes.meaning: Limit switch open; downflow note calls out auxiliary limit open on downflow-only applications
root_causes: Airflow restriction, blower issue, dirty filter, or overfire
diagnostic_path: Check limit continuity and application-specific auxiliary limits
If remade, confirm airflow and temperature rise
codes.escalation: If limit repeats, move to airflow / blower / input check.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when limit remakes and heat cycle is reattempted.

root_causes: Contaminated flame rod or grounding issues can contribute
diagnostic_path: Verify flame is not present with valve de-energized
Inspect flame-sense circuit and gas valve behavior
codes.escalation: Escalate quickly because false flame can mask unsafe gas-valve faults.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when abnormal flame signal is removed.

codes.meaning: Control in soft lockout (automatic restart or retry delay)
diagnostic_path: Pull preceding active fault from call sequence if visible
Verify gas supply, pilot ignition, pressure proving, and limits
codes.escalation: Escalate to full ignition-sequence diagnosis if recurring.
codes.reset_behavior: Automatic restart/retry delay per OEM wording.
codes.source: OEM code table and sequence text
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: On ICP Gen 2/3, Code 6 is active ignition/flame-proving failure (different meaning). On ICP Recent IGC, Code 6 is narrower ignition proving failure. On ICP SmartValve legacy, Code 6 is broader.

root_causes: Tubing/condensate problems can hold switch proved
diagnostic_path: Verify switch is open at heat call start
Inspect switch/tubing/wiring
codes.escalation: Replace switch only after tubing and wiring are ruled out.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when switch state is correct on next call.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: On ICP Gen 2/3, Code 8 is gas-heating permanent lockout (completely different).

root_causes: Inducer performance, vent restriction, or tubing issues
diagnostic_path: Verify high-fire proving path during high-stage transition
Inspect tubing and venting
codes.escalation: Escalate to inducer/vent draft diagnosis if switch remains open.
codes.reset_behavior: Retries after correction.

platforms.platform_description: SmartValve-era staged furnace platform using FCB diagnostic light and 6+X soft-lockout subcode architecture. Precursor to Gen 3 (F/G)9MVE variable-speed ECM platform. More granular subcodes than Gen 1 NTV6.
platforms.visual_identification: FCB (furnace control board) diagnostic light with 6+X soft-lockout subcodes (6+1, 6+2, 6+3, 6+4). Distinct from Gen 1 NTV6 which uses gas-valve diagnostic light instead.
platforms.platform_notes: 6+X soft-lockout architecture with 5-minute retry delay. Code 7 transitions to 1-hour soft lockout. Code 8 = pressure switch fault (different from Gen 3 where Code 8 = gas heating lockout).

codes.meaning: OFF = no 24/115V or open fuse; Heartbeat = normal/no previous diagnostic; ON SOLID = soft lockout, furnace control error
diagnostic_path: Verify line voltage, 24V, fuse, and control if ON SOLID repeats after reset
codes.escalation: Replace control if ON SOLID repeats immediately after power reset.
codes.reset_behavior: ON SOLID = 1-hour delay in verified table.

codes.meaning: Pressure switch(es) closed when should be open
diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before call
Inspect tubing and wiring
Remove jumpers/miswires
codes.escalation: Replace switch only after proving circuit checks.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when proper open state is restored.
codes.source: OEM FCB list and SmartValve troubleshooting table

codes.meaning: Low-pressure switch open when should be closed
diagnostic_path: Check switch operation, tubing, wiring, and restrictions in air intake/vent piping
codes.escalation: Escalate to inducer/vent diagnosis if proving never establishes.
codes.reset_behavior: System enters 5-minute delay, then begins another cycle.
codes.source: OEM FCB table and troubleshooting check/repair list

codes.meaning: Limit or flame roll-out switch open
root_causes: Airflow or blower problems sit behind it
diagnostic_path: Check main limit, rollout, and wiring continuity
If repeated, verify airflow and duct restriction
codes.escalation: Manual-reset rollout demands flame-path/combustion review before return to service.
codes.reset_behavior: If open less than 2 minutes, stays as active fault rather than long lockout.

diagnostic_path: Verify no flame with valve de-energized
Inspect gas valve and flame-sense circuit
codes.escalation: Escalate promptly for gas-valve verification.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when false flame condition is removed.

codes.meaning: Failure to ignite or flame sense lost while running
diagnostic_path: Follow OEM gas supply, HSI, earth ground, flame rod position/cleanliness, pilot position, pressure switch/tubing/wiring, and condensate drain blockage path
codes.escalation: If it accumulates to 6+1/6+2/6+3/6+4, follow last-failure subcode.
codes.reset_behavior: Active fault before soft-lockout escalation.
codes.source: OEM FCB list and SmartValve troubleshooting matrix

codes.meaning: Max four trials for ignition reached
root_causes: Failed light-off: gas, igniter, pilot/flame sense, grounding
diagnostic_path: Follow OEM ignition checks first: gas supply, HSI/pilot, flame rod, ground
codes.escalation: Replace failed ignition component only after proving gas and control sequence.
codes.reset_behavior: 3-hour delay; after delay system retries if call remains.
codes.source: OEM subcode table and heating-with-gas-shutoff sequence

codes.meaning: Max recycle count exceeded; last failure was flame sense lost during run
diagnostic_path: Focus on flame-sense microamps, ground path, pilot stability, gas pressure stability
codes.escalation: Move to control/gas-valve review if flame current and burner stability are normal.
codes.reset_behavior: 5-minute delay then reset/initiate new ignition sequence.

codes.meaning: Max recycle count exceeded; last failure was pressure-switch failure during run
diagnostic_path: Verify proving switches under live run conditions
Inspect condensate and vent path
codes.escalation: Escalate to inducer and venting analysis if intermittent.
codes.reset_behavior: 5-minute delay then retry.

codes.meaning: Max recycle count exceeded; last failure was limit circuit opened during run
root_causes: Main limit or rollout opened during run
diagnostic_path: Check limit/rollout continuity
Then airflow, filter, duct restriction, blower
codes.escalation: Any rollout trip demands combustion/burner review before reset.
codes.reset_behavior: 5-minute delay then retry.

codes.meaning: Limit or rollout switch open longer than 2 minutes
root_causes: Restriction in ductwork, dirty filter, dead blower, blocked ductwork
diagnostic_path: Check limit/rollout, wiring, blower operation, filter, duct restriction
codes.escalation: Rollout = manual reset and combustion investigation.
codes.reset_behavior: 1-hour delay; system starts new ignition sequence after 1 hour if call remains.
codes.source: OEM FCB table and check/repair list

codes.meaning: High-pressure switch closed when should be open
diagnostic_path: Verify switch open at call start
Inspect tubing and wiring
codes.escalation: Replace switch only after proving circuit checks.
codes.reset_behavior: Waits for switch to open before energizing combustion blower.

codes.meaning: High-pressure switch open when should be closed
diagnostic_path: Verify high-fire proving path and vent/inducer operation
codes.escalation: Escalate to high-fire inducer/vent analysis if intermittent.
codes.reset_behavior: 5-minute delay, then another cycle.

codes.meaning: Improper transformer phasing on twinned applications or improper line-voltage polarity
diagnostic_path: Confirm line polarity and transformer phasing on twinned furnaces
codes.escalation: Rework wiring/twinning phasing before condemning board.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when wiring fault is corrected.

platforms.platform_description: Latest ICP noncommunicating two-stage variable-speed ECM board platform. 35-inch high-efficiency condensing gas furnace. Uses service-label flow chart with compound codes like 1+2, 2+5, 4+3 plus 6+1 ignition lockout subcode.
platforms.visual_identification: Service label flow chart with OFF, HEARTBEAT, ON SOLID plus numeric codes and compound codes (1+2, 2+5, 4+3, 6+1). SW1-1 for history recall, SW1-6 for component self-test mode.
platforms.platform_notes: Component self-test mode (SW1-6 ON) runs: inducer high-speed, HSI 15 sec, blower 15 sec, inducer low-speed 10 sec, then post-test indication. Non-modulating board requires software version V24 or later for 2+5 setup code support.

diagnostic_path: Verify normal operation
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Steady while normal.
codes.source: ICP (F/G)9MVE A3/A4 Service Manual Figure 15 Service Label

diagnostic_path: Inspect 24VAC circuit for shorts before replacing fuse
Replace fuse and disconnect thermostat leads to isolate short circuit
codes.escalation: Escalate only after isolating field low-voltage wiring and accessories if fuse continues to open.
codes.reset_behavior: Restores when short is corrected and fuse is replaced.
codes.source: ICP (F/G)9MVE A3/A4 Service Manual Figure 15 Service Label and Figure 17 Troubleshooting Guide

codes.meaning: Control locked out, auto-reset after 1 hour, possible gas valve relay stuck open / flame sense circuit failure / software check error
diagnostic_path: Verify 115V, 24V at SEC-1/SEC-2, fuse, and model plug
If ON SOLID repeats after reset, condemn control
codes.escalation: Replace control if repeating immediately after reset.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-reset after 1 hour for ON SOLID.

diagnostic_path: Verify switch open before call
Inspect tubing and switch
codes.escalation: Replace switch only after tubing inspection.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when normal open state returns.

codes.meaning: Low-heat pressure switch did not close or reopened
root_causes: Low inlet gas pressure if LGPS used
diagnostic_path: Run proving circuit live at low heat and work through OEM list above
codes.escalation: Escalate to inducer or vent system if switch and tubing check good.
codes.reset_behavior: If open longer than 5 minutes inducer shuts off for 15 minutes before retry.

codes.meaning: Limit or flame rollout switch open, or furnace forced into high-heat-only mode due to two successive low-heat limit trips
diagnostic_path: Check whether it is actual open limit/rollout versus high-heat-only fallback
Then check airflow and gas input
codes.escalation: Rollout requires manual reset and combustion review.
codes.reset_behavior: If open >3 minutes changes to code 7; if shorter, code 4 continues until blower shuts off.

codes.meaning: Abnormal flame-proving signal; flame proved while gas valve is de-energized
diagnostic_path: Verify no flame with gas valve de-energized
Inspect valve leakage
codes.escalation: Gas valve safety fault, not just a sensor nuisance.
codes.reset_behavior: Inducer runs until fault clears.

codes.meaning: Flame not sensed during trial for ignition, or flame signal lost during blower-on delay
root_causes: Missing green/yellow ground wire to furnace sheet metal
diagnostic_path: OEM sequence: component test for igniter
Verify 115V to igniter
Verify 24V to gas valve
Observe burner ignition/stability
Measure flame current
codes.escalation: If four attempts fail, progresses to 6+1.
codes.reset_behavior: Repeats ignition sequence 3 more times before 6+1 lockout.

codes.meaning: System failed to ignite gas and prove flame in 4 attempts
root_causes: Same buckets as code 6, unresolved through four tries
diagnostic_path: Refer back to code 6 root cause chain
codes.escalation: Control/valve/HSI/flame-sense resolution required before return.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-reset after 3 hours.

codes.meaning: Limit or rollout open longer than 3 minutes, or 10 successive limit trips occurred during high heat
root_causes: Same underlying causes as code 4, but persistent/repetitive
diagnostic_path: Work code 4 chain first, then determine why fault persisted or repeated
codes.escalation: Persistent lockout = airflow/gas input/limit integrity review before release.
codes.reset_behavior: Auto-reset after 3 hours.

root_causes: Stuck-closed gas valve relay on control
diagnostic_path: Verify valve not being backfed or miswired
Verify control relay state
codes.escalation: Hard safety/electrical isolation issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Does not auto-reset; power off and wait 5 minutes to retry.

codes.meaning: High-heat pressure switch or relay did not close or reopened
root_causes: Low-heat proving issues carried forward from code 3
diagnostic_path: Check high-fire proving path and refer to code 3 chain
codes.escalation: Escalate to control relay or high-stage proving circuit if low-stage proves normally.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when proving path is restored.

codes.meaning: Polarity fault; if twinned, also check low-voltage transformer phasing
diagnostic_path: Check line polarity first, then twinning phasing if applicable
codes.escalation: Correct supply wiring before deeper board diagnosis.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears after wiring correction.

codes.meaning: Blower on after power up (115VAC or 24VAC). Blower runs 90 seconds if unit powered up during call for heat, or when R-W/W1 opens during blower on-delay period.
root_causes: Not a fault; typical triggers are power restoration during active heat call, or loss/opening of heat call during blower on-delay period
diagnostic_path: Verify whether power was just restored during heat call, or whether R-W/W1 opened during blower on-delay
If yes, treat as normal 90-second blower-only operation
codes.escalation: No escalation if behavior matches OEM conditions and clears after 90-second interval.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clearing. After 90-second period, LED returns to heartbeat if no faults detected.
codes.source: ICP Service and Technical Support Manual, Gas Furnace: (F/G)9MVE A3/A4, Figure 15 Service Label and Figure 17 / Sequence of Operation

codes.meaning: MODEL SELECTION OR SETUP ERROR. Either model plug (PL4) is missing or incorrect, or setup switch SW1-1 or SW1-6 is positioned improperly.
root_causes: SW1-1 left ON after code recall
SW1-6 left ON after component test
diagnostic_path: Confirm correct PL4 is installed
Check whether code flashed only 4 times at power-up or flashes continuously
Verify SW1-1 is OFF unless intentionally retrieving history
Verify SW1-6 is OFF unless intentionally running component test
Check for mismatched twinning
Confirm replacement board type and software level
codes.escalation: Escalate to board/parts verification if PL4 is correct, switches are correctly positioned, twinning is valid, and code persists.
codes.reset_behavior: No timed auto-reset. Clear by correcting model-plug/setup condition. In component-test context: turn SW1-6 OFF and then back ON to repeat test.
codes.source: ICP (F/G)9MVE A3/A4 Service Manual Figure 15 Service Label, Figure 17 Troubleshooting Guide, and Component Self-Test section
codes.field_consensus_notes: Unique to Gen 3 platform — not present on Gen 1 NTV6 or Gen 2 *9MPT/*9MPV. Requires non-modulating board software version V24 or later.

codes.meaning: Low-heat pressure switch open while high-heat pressure switch is closed
root_causes: Low inlet gas pressure if LGPS used
Water in vent piping, possibly sagging pipe
diagnostic_path: Check condensate drainage first
Verify inlet gas pressure if low-gas-pressure switch is used
Inspect pressure-switch wiring
Inspect vent piping for water/sagging
Inspect pressure tubing for disconnection or obstruction
Test low-heat pressure switch for open/stuck-open condition
codes.escalation: Escalate to control/pressure-proving circuit diagnosis only after condensate, venting, tubing, wiring, gas-pressure, and low-heat switch condition are verified.
codes.reset_behavior: No distinct timed auto-reset; treat as active status that should clear when contradictory pressure-switch condition is corrected.
codes.source: ICP (F/G)9MVE A3/A4 Service Manual Figure 15 Service Label
codes.field_consensus_notes: Unique to Gen 3 platform.

codes.meaning: Post-component-test indication: no errors detected. Visual check of hot surface igniter, inducer, and blower motor required.
root_causes: Normal post-component-test result when no setup error is present and visual component sequence completes
diagnostic_path: Run component test with R disconnected and SW1-6 ON
Observe inducer high-speed, igniter ON, blower ON, then inducer low-speed
If heartbeat is post-test indication, verify visually that those components actually operated
codes.escalation: No escalation if observed sequence matches OEM component-test sequence. Escalate only if heartbeat appears but a component visibly failed to operate.
codes.reset_behavior: After testing, put SW1-6 in OFF position and reconnect R thermostat lead.
codes.source: ICP (F/G)9MVE A3/A4 Service Manual Component Self-Test section and Figure 15 Service Label component-test box

codes.meaning: SETUP ERROR during component test — same as normal code 2+5
root_causes: Same as normal code 2+5: PL4/model selection error or improper switch/setup state
diagnostic_path: Correct setup/model issue per normal 2+5 diagnostics
To repeat component test, turn SW1-6 OFF and then back ON
codes.escalation: Escalate to board/model-plug verification if setup looks correct but indication remains.
codes.reset_behavior: To repeat component test, turn SW1-6 OFF and then back ON. After component test completed, put SW1-6 in OFF position and reconnect R thermostat lead.
codes.source: ICP (F/G)9MVE A3/A4 Service Manual Figure 15 Service Label component-test box and Component Self-Test section

platforms.platform_name: Carrier Infinity / Bryant Evolution Communicating Systems
platforms.platform_description: Carrier Infinity / Bryant Evolution communicating ecosystem using Connex wall control and ABCD communication bus. Includes two-part wall-control codes (e.g., 41-13, 88-55), inverter/VFD code families (82/84/87/88), and equipment-board Status Code 16 variants on fan coils and outdoor units. Dual-surface platform — codes may appear at wall control, equipment board, or both. 186CNV/284ANV inverter outdoor uses COMM light / matrix display instead of traditional flash codes.
applies_to_models: Communicating fan coils: FE4A, FE5A, FV4C
Connex wall controls: SYSTXCCNNC, SYSTXCCITC, SYSTXBBECC
platforms.visual_identification: Infinity or Evolution branded wall control with color touchscreen (Connex) OR simple older Infinity Touch. System speaks on ABCD communication bus. Outdoor unit may have COMM light, VFD Comm light, amber Status LED, and matrix display (on inverter platforms). Fan coils show Status Code 16 on amber board LED.
platforms.platform_notes: Three distinct display surfaces: (1) Connex wall control shows two-part codes like 41-13, 88-55; (2) Equipment board amber LED on fan coils and legacy communicating outdoor shows Status Code 16 variants; (3) 186CNV/284ANV inverter outdoor shows COMM light state and matrix display. Same fault can surface on multiple displays. Model plug critical for board replacements.

codes.meaning: Defrost timeout / defrost cycle did not complete properly
diagnostic_path: Verify reversing valve operation
Verify outdoor coil thermistor
Check refrigerant charge
Inspect outdoor fan during defrost cycle
codes.escalation: Persistent defrost timeouts with verified sensors indicate refrigeration or reversing-valve issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when defrost completes successfully on next attempt.

diagnostic_path: Verify PEV solenoid operation
Inspect for refrigerant-line restrictions
codes.escalation: Replace PEV only after confirming wiring and supply integrity.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when PEV completes equalization on retry.

diagnostic_path: Measure OAT sensor resistance
Verify wiring continuity to board
codes.escalation: Replace sensor after wiring confirmed good.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when sensor reading returns to valid range.
codes.source: Carrier inverter/Connex service manual event tables

diagnostic_path: Measure OAT sensor resistance
Inspect wiring for shorts

codes.meaning: System Communication Fault on communicating fan coil (FE4A/FE5A/FV4C). If system communications unsuccessful for >2 minutes, fan coil displays Status Code 16 and drops into emergency-operable state that can only run with common thermostat wiring and non-communicating outdoor unit.

diagnostic_path: Check fan coil COMM LED — should light during startup
Verify UI is powered
Inspect ABCD wiring end-to-end
Confirm conductor-to-conductor continuity and no shorts
Verify correct polarity/landing
codes.escalation: Public docs allow temporary emergency heating/cooling fallback using common heat/cool thermostat wired to R, Y, W, C and non-communicating outdoor unit — temporary measure only.
codes.reset_behavior: Not a manual-reset lockout. Local board indication clears when communications are restored. Emergency-mode operation remains available while board is powered and displaying 16.
codes.source: Carrier FV4C Service Manual, FE4A/FE5A installation instructions, Carrier fan-coil wiring diagram/troubleshooting legend, Sigler NorCal public master fault code list.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Equipment-board primary surface; fault also surfaces at wall control as system communication event but local board presentation is the amber STATUS LED showing 16.

codes.reset_behavior: Clears when communication is re-established. Not documented as a manual-reset outdoor-board lockout.
codes.source: Carrier/Bryant 24ANB communicating outdoor service manual status code table, 25VNA service manual communication-loss section, Sigler NorCal public master fault code list.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Equipment-board primary surface; same underlying failure also visible at wall control.

codes.meaning: If COMM light is OFF, communication has not been received within the last 2 minutes and equipment is operating in emergency communicating mode. Public 186CNV/284ANV service manual documents this board-level interpretation — NOT a traditional Status Code 16 flash code.
root_causes: Loss of comms from communicating control
Device/network fault preventing valid communication messages within last 2 minutes
diagnostic_path: Inspect COMM light at outdoor unit — ON = normal communication, OFF = emergency communicating mode
Inspect VFD Comm light — blinks with message traffic, green for error-free, red for errors/missing messages
Board's matrix display can scroll active diagnostic codes
codes.escalation: Platform's outdoor logic designed to continue in emergency communicating mode when comm messages haven't been received within last 2 minutes. Restore communicating path.
codes.reset_behavior: Returns to normal communication mode when valid communication resumes. Public manual frames this as mode-state change based on whether comm received within last 2 minutes, not as conventional manual-reset outdoor flash-code lockout.
codes.source: Carrier/Bryant 186CNV/284ANV service manual COMM-light and VFD Comm-light descriptions, public 24VNA0/25VNA0 service manual event table showing Event 16 communication loss, Sigler NorCal public master fault code list.
codes.field_consensus_notes: NOT a traditional board flash code. Public documentation does not cleanly show a conventional outdoor-board Status Code 16 presentation for 186CNV/284ANV. Board-level indicator is COMM light state and matrix display behavior. Same underlying fault surfaces at wall control as communication-loss event family.

codes.meaning: Wall control cannot find indoor unit on ABCD communication bus during initial Connex discovery/commissioning. COMMISSIONING-TIME DIAGNOSTIC — not a runtime fault. Appears during initial system setup, not during normal operation.
root_causes: ABCD wiring not connected between wall control and indoor unit
Indoor unit power not available during discovery
Wrong order of unit power-up during commissioning
ABCD polarity reversed (A swapped with B)
Failed indoor board preventing communication at startup
diagnostic_path: Verify 24V power at indoor unit board
Verify ABCD wiring from wall control to indoor unit, correct polarity and landing
Verify no shorts, opens, or high resistance on comm conductors
Retry Connex discovery sequence from wall control
If indoor still not found, escalate to indoor board diagnosis
codes.escalation: If wiring and power confirmed but indoor still not discovered, indoor board failure becomes likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when indoor unit is successfully discovered during next commissioning attempt.
codes.source: Carrier/Bryant Infinity/Evolution Connex wall control installation instructions — commissioning/discovery section.
codes.field_consensus_notes: COMMISSIONING-ONLY. Keep separate from runtime fault dictionary — techs troubleshooting a running system should not find this in runtime fault history.

codes.meaning: Wall control cannot find outdoor unit on ABCD communication bus during initial Connex discovery/commissioning. COMMISSIONING-TIME DIAGNOSTIC — not a runtime fault. Appears during initial system setup, not during normal operation.
root_causes: ABCD wiring not connected between indoor unit/wall control and outdoor unit
Outdoor unit disconnect not turned on during discovery
Outdoor unit power not yet established (e.g., 24V transformer issue)
ABCD polarity reversed at outdoor unit
Open or shorted ABCD conductors between indoor and outdoor
Failed outdoor board or VFD preventing communication at startup
Wrong model plug installed preventing outdoor from responding properly
diagnostic_path: Verify outdoor disconnect is ON and line voltage is present
Verify 24V power at outdoor control board
Verify ABCD wiring from indoor to outdoor with correct polarity
Verify correct model plug is installed on outdoor board
Verify no shorts, opens, or high resistance on ABCD between indoor and outdoor
On 186CNV/284ANV inverter outdoor, check COMM light and VFD Comm light state
Retry Connex discovery sequence
codes.escalation: If wiring, power, and model plug verified but outdoor still not discovered, outdoor board or VFD failure becomes likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when outdoor unit is successfully discovered during next commissioning attempt.
codes.source: Carrier/Bryant Infinity/Evolution Connex wall control installation instructions — commissioning/discovery section.
codes.field_consensus_notes: COMMISSIONING-ONLY. Keep separate from runtime fault dictionary. Model-plug issues are a common cause on board replacements during commissioning.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: Code 6 has four distinct meanings across Carrier Global platforms. On ICP Recent/Intermediate IGC (N9MSB/N9MSE): ignition proving failure — retries 3 more times before 6+1 lockout. On ICP SmartValve legacy single-stage: failure to ignite OR flame sense lost while running (broader, active before 6+X soft lockout). On ICP Gen 1 NTV6/NTP6/NDP6/VNE two-stage: soft lockout status (automatic restart/retry delay, NOT active ignition fault). On ICP Gen 2/3 (*9MPT/*9MPV and (F/G)9MVE): active ignition/flame-proving failure before 6+1 lockout.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Identify platform before diagnosing. Legacy SmartValve and Gen 1 two-stage treat Code 6 very differently from Recent IGC and Gen 2/3 staged. The 6+X subcode architecture only exists on SmartValve legacy, Gen 2, and Gen 3.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: Code 8 means gas heating lockout on ICP Recent IGC and ICP Gen 2/3 staged (stuck-closed gas valve relay, miswire, or short to gas valve wire). On ICP Gen 1 NTV6/NTP6/NDP6/VNE, Code 8 = high-pressure switch stuck closed — completely different diagnostic path (pressure-switch circuit, not gas valve).
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Confirm platform generation first. Gen 1 staged/variable Code 8 requires pressure-switch investigation; Recent IGC and Gen 2/3 Code 8 requires gas-valve/board investigation.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: Code 16 has three distinct meanings across Infinity/Evolution communicating equipment. On communicating fan coils (FE4A/FE5A/FV4C): System Communication Fault — fan coil has lost comm for >2 minutes and drops into emergency-operable mode. On legacy communicating outdoor (24ANB/25HCB pre-inverter): Communication with User Interface lost. On Extreme/Greenspeed inverter (24VNA6/25VNA4 wall-control event): Communication Loss event number. Additionally, on 186CNV/284ANV inverter outdoor, Code 16 does NOT appear as a traditional board flash code — the equivalent indicator is COMM light OFF.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Identify which equipment is showing the 16. Fan coil vs legacy outdoor vs inverter outdoor all differ in troubleshooting approach. On 186CNV/284ANV specifically, look for COMM light state rather than a flash code.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On Carrier/Bryant/Payne legacy 58MVP/58MVC: Code 23 = pressure switch did not open (switch stuck closed). On Carrier 58SC0B newer 3-digit platform: Code 23.1 = stuck main pressure switch (same underlying fault, but decimal format matters for platform identification).
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: The decimal in 23.1 is the platform identifier. Tool must key on exact format including decimal, not just base number.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On Carrier 58MVP/58MVC legacy variable-speed: Code 31 = high-heat pressure switch or relay did not close/reopened (tied to staged two-stage logic). On Carrier 58SC0B newer single-stage: Code 31.1 = open main pressure switch / pressure switch did not function properly (single-stage broader meaning).
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Legacy 58MVP handles this as high-heat-specific staging logic; 58SC0B is a broader general pressure-switch fault since it's single-stage equipment.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On Carrier 58MVP/58MVC legacy: Code 45 = control circuitry lockout (broad diagnosis, replace control if repeated). On Carrier 58SC0B newer: Split into 45.1 (flame circuit fault/memory mismatch), 45.2 (gas valve relay won't close), and 45.3 (EEPROM memory issue) — much more granular.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: 58SC0B subcodes (45.1, 45.2, 45.3) give specific failure mode. Legacy 58MVP 45 is blanket 'replace control' guidance.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On ICP SmartValve legacy single-stage (N8MPN family): Code 6+1 = soft lockout, max ignition trials reached — 5-minute delay then retry. On ICP Gen 2 staged SmartValve (*9MPT/*9MPV): Code 6+1 = ignition lockout after 4 trials — 3-hour delay. Both are ignition-failure lockouts but with different reset behaviors.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Platform determines whether 6+1 clears in 5 minutes or 3 hours. Tell customer accordingly.

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On ICP Recent/Intermediate IGC: Code 7 = limit circuit lockout after limit/rollout open >3 minutes. Auto-reset after 3 hours. On ICP SmartValve legacy: Code 7 = soft lockout after limit/rollout open >2 minutes. 1-hour delay. On ICP Gen 2/3 staged: Code 7 = limit circuit lockout. Auto-reset after 3 hours. Timing thresholds differ across generations.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: All Code 7 events are limit-related, but the timing threshold and reset behavior differ by platform.

electric_heat_notes.conventional_electric_furnace_codes: Carrier/Bryant/Payne and ICP-branded air handlers use simpler electric-heat element and sequencer logic. Public manuals rarely document a formal electric-heat fault code dictionary. Most electric-heat service calls are for component-level failures (element burnout, sequencer stuck, limit trip, open transformer) without specific fault codes. Communicating fan coils (FE4A/FE5A/FV4C) may show Status Code 16 if electric-heat operation is attempted during a communication failure that forces emergency mode.
common_non_code_electric_service_calls: Open element (one or more of 3-5 elements in a typical 10-20 kW package)
Miswired or failed thermostat staging on multi-stage heat
Contactor coil failure on high-kW packages
electric_heat_notes.manufactured_home_specifics: Not applicable — Carrier Global brands (except some ICP models) do not primarily serve manufactured-home market.
